# yap

> yap is a lightweight, open-source voice-to-text CLI tool for Linux, macOS, and Windows.

## What it does

yap converts speech to text at your cursor using a hold-to-talk workflow.
Hold a hotkey, speak, release — the transcribed text appears wherever your cursor is.

## Key facts

- Written in Go, ships as a single static binary
- Uses ~5–10 MB RAM when idle, negligible CPU
- Transcription via Groq Whisper API (~1–2s latency)
- Configurable via TOML, first-class NixOS module available
- Zero telemetry, no analytics, no phone-home
- Bring your own API key — $0 software cost
- MIT licensed

## Links

- Website: https://yap.enriquefft.com
- Source: https://github.com/enriquefft/yap
- Install: curl -fsSL https://yap.sh/install | bash
- Issues: https://github.com/enriquefft/yap/issues

## Alternatives

yap is an alternative to macOS Dictation, Windows Voice Typing, Talon Voice,
Whisper.cpp local transcription, and Nerd Dictation. It differs by being
a minimal CLI with zero idle footprint and cloud-based transcription.
